Home›Federal Services› SOC Design, Implementation & Operations
Federal Cybersecurity Operations

Federal SOC Design,
Implementation & Operations

From architecture through continuous operations — we design, build, staff, and operate cybersecurity operations teams for DoD contractors, federal agencies, and CMMC-regulated organizations.

Practical Experience Building Federal Cyber Operations

Standing up a security operations capability in a federal or DoD-adjacent environment requires more than technology selection — it demands deep understanding of regulatory requirements, authorization frameworks, classification boundaries, and the operational realities of protecting controlled unclassified information (CUI) and federal systems. We bring hands-on experience in designing and operating these environments, not just advising on them.

Phase I — Design & Architecture

Every effective federal SOC begins with architecture that aligns technical requirements with regulatory obligations. We conduct a comprehensive baseline assessment and design a security operations architecture that addresses your specific environment — whether you're a DoD prime contractor, subcontractor, federal agency, or commercial organization pursuing federal contracts.

  • Current-state security posture assessment and gap analysis against NIST SP 800-53 and CMMC requirements
  • Security operations architecture design — on-premise, cloud, or hybrid
  • SIEM platform selection and scoping (Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel, Elastic, Chronicle)
  • Log source inventory, collection architecture, and data normalization planning
  • Detection use case library development aligned to MITRE ATT&CK for ICS/Enterprise
  • Staffing model design — internal analysts, co-managed, or fully outsourced
  • Authorization boundary definition for DISA STIG and RMF alignment

Phase II — Implementation

We deploy, configure, and validate every component of the security operations stack — then validate it against your regulatory requirements before operational handoff.

  • SIEM deployment, log ingestion, and data model configuration
  • EDR deployment and policy configuration across endpoints and servers
  • Network security monitoring sensor placement and tuning
  • Detection rule deployment, tuning, and false positive reduction
  • SOAR integration and playbook development for common incident types
  • Analyst workflow design and escalation playbook documentation
  • Security operations runbook development for Tier 1/2/3 response procedures
  • RMF ATO support — artifact generation, control documentation, and assessor coordination

Phase III — Operations & Maturity

Standing up the SOC is only the beginning. Sustained security operations require continuous improvement, threat intelligence integration, and program maturity progression over time.

  • Co-managed or fully managed SOC operations — 24/7 alert monitoring and triage
  • Monthly threat intelligence integration and detection coverage review
  • Quarterly red team exercises and purple team detection validation
  • CMMC assessment readiness support and evidence collection
  • Continuous monitoring (ConMon) program management for ATO maintenance
  • Analyst training, tabletop exercises, and incident response drills
  • POA&M tracking and remediation program management

Regulatory & Framework Alignment

Our SOC designs are built with the following frameworks as primary references:

  • CMMC Level 2 & 3 — Full practice coverage across all 14 domains for organizations handling CUI
  • NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 — SI, IR, AU, CA, and RA control families implemented and documented
  • NIST SP 800-171 — Contractor-facing implementation for protecting CUI in non-federal systems
  • DFARS 252.204-7012 — Rapid reporting obligations and cloud service provider compliance
  • RMF (NIST SP 800-37) — Authorization package development and ATO support
  • MITRE ATT&CK — Detection coverage mapping, threat modeling, and red team alignment

Engagement Phases

  • Design & Architecture
  • Implementation
  • Operations & Maturity
  • ConMon & ATO Support

Frameworks

  • CMMC Level 2 & 3
  • NIST SP 800-53
  • NIST SP 800-171
  • DFARS 7012
  • RMF / ATO
  • MITRE ATT&CK

Technology Stack

  • Splunk Enterprise / SOAR
  • Microsoft Sentinel
  • CrowdStrike Falcon
  • SentinelOne
  • Elastic Security
  • Tenable / Nessus
